description: A plain candid 'perp-walk' style operational photograph with no graphic design or on-image slogans (only text visible is printed on equipment in the scene). It is shot indoors in what appears to be a vehicle bay or garage. In the center stands a man in a light gray crewneck sweatshirt and olive pants, arms held behind him, flanked by two officers in dark tactical gear and ball caps, both wearing black balaclavas/face coverings; the left officer's vest reads 'POLICE' and the right officer's vest reads 'POLICE' with an ICE/HSI badge patch. Behind them is a large dark armored tactical vehicle (BearCat-type) with 'POLICE', 'RESCUE', and 'SPECIAL RESPONSE TEAM' lettering visible. The image presents a detained subject (per the tweet, Salvadoran national Francisco Reyes-Ramirez). There is no designed overlay, headline, or meme styling, so it is documentary rather than a designed graphic, though it is composed in the now-common ICE 'two masked officers flanking a detainee' arrangement.
notable_text: POLICE; RESCUE; SPECIAL RESPONSE TEAM (lettering on gear/armored vehicle)
